meaning: n.靠股息生活的人,靠收租生活的人,<贬>食利者
examples: After all, Keynes talked about the "euthanasia of the rentier".
And oil importers, who rely on remittances and aid from oil exporting countries, have all the characteristics of a rentier state-without the resources.
Given all this, most sane New Yorkers would rather eat their money than join the rentier class.
As technology proceeds in a patent-obsessed world, the fruits of innovation flow to the owners of the capital and invention, forming a whole new rentier class.
You are in a minority here in America and, I imagine, everywhere else where people do not have the economic mentality of a prissy, doddering rentier.
